sources.notes: Statement and hack format: learn an unknown ordered min-max function; easy version has n <= 70 and a loose query budget.
Used to confirm the H1 divide-and-conquer reconstruction of an ordered alternating min/max tree.
Chinese solution consulted to cross-check the shared H1/H2/H3 min-max tree model and split-finding approach.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution index; the article listed in sources was the actually consulted Luogu source.

annotation.summary: Learn an unknown ordered min-max expression over x1..xn using oracle-style queries, then answer evaluation queries; in the hack format the hidden function is supplied to the interactor.
annotation.constraints: Easy version: n <= 70, sum n <= 70, and a generous learning-query limit.
annotation.core_idea: Model the expression as an ordered rooted tree whose internal nodes are min/max and leaves are x1..xn in order. With 0/1 or strictly ordered query vectors, the returned value identifies which leaf wins under chosen defaults. Increasing/decreasing probes determine the top operation and localize a split f=min(g,h) or f=max(g,h). In H1, the split can be found by direct scanning under the loose budget; recurse on the two intervals using neutral extremes outside the active interval.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2) oracle queries is sufficient for H1; later evaluation is O(n) on the reconstructed tree.
tricks: 0-1 sorting-style probes identify the winning leaf
neutral infinities isolate a recursive interval
linear split search is acceptable only in the easy version

solution_variants.name: quadratic-divide-and-conquer-reconstruction
solution_variants.summary: Recursively determine the root operation and a split point using ordered probes and linear split search, building an equivalent min-max tree.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n^2) oracle queries, O(n) per final evaluation query

tags.evidence: The central task is to design oracle-style queries that reconstruct an unknown min-max function before answering evaluations.
tags.description: Interactive or query-based problems where the main goal is to reconstruct an unknown hidden structure or function from adaptive oracle answers.
tags.created_reason: The broad interactive tag does not distinguish reusable hidden-structure reconstruction from simple interactive search or games.

tags.evidence: After finding the top split, the same reconstruction problem is solved recursively on the left and right variable intervals.
The original model is adaptive interaction; the hack format still preserves the hidden-function reconstruction pattern.
The unknown expression is represented and evaluated as an ordered alternating min/max tree.
Query answers guide the reconstruction decisions for the hidden expression tree.
